runs.summary: Data through Jul 2 (Q2 now complete). Top 3: SMH +82.1%, XLK +32.7%, CIBR +26.1%. SPY P/E: 26.20x (preserved)
runs.content:
Top 5 YTD 2026: SMH +82.1% (P/E 52.06x, Expensive), XLK +32.7% (39.50x, Expensive), XBI +29.9% (P/E N/A), CIBR +26.1% (33.06x, Elevated), ICLN +25.0% (24.98x, Fair).

Bottom 5 YTD 2026: GDX -12.0% (13.66x, Cheap), XLC -8.5% (15.80x, Fair), GLD -7.1% (P/E N/A), XLY -1.4% (29.53x, Elevated), XLF -1.3% (17.13x, Fair).

Notable shifts: XRT crossed P/E Fair→Cheap (15.78x→14.80x), the only valuation band crossing this run. SMH and XLK both posted unusually large Q2 finishes (+71.1% and +43.5% for the quarter) pushing YTD past their full 2025 totals — flagged as a data-quality item since P/E for both held flat despite the price move. XLE gave back a third of its huge Q1 gain in Q2 (-12.7%), downgraded from Breakout to Consolidating momentum. ICLN's momentum recovered from Consolidating back to Continuing as the complete Q2 print beat the prior partial estimate. GDX/GLD deepened their 2026 reversals.
